arts.ttl: Reading boss looks ahead to tricky tie at 'dogged and physically strong' Blackpool
arts.sum: Reading will need to overcome 'dogged and physically strong' Blackpool tonight if they are to extend their unbeaten run to 12 matches. The Royals are on their best run since 2012 and moved up to sixth in League One following Saturday's 3-1 victory over Peterborough United. Steve Bruce's Blackpool won 3-0 in Hunt's first home match in charge in December, while Bloomfield Road is a ground the Royals have not won a league match at since 2001. But in order to extend the unbeaten run and keep up their play-off charge, the Tangerines will need to be beaten.

arts.ttl: Why Reading could be kicked out of the EFL - takeover turmoil explained
arts.sum: Reading were once seen as the benchmark of how to run a football club - now they face an existential crisis after 153 years of history. Here's why…

arts.ttl: Former Reading star pays emotional tribute after sudden passing of brother
arts.sum: Former Reading midfielder Andy Rinomhota has paid an emotional tribute to his brother following his tragic passing in Thailand. The 27-year-old rose through the ranks with the Royals, making over 100 appearances and winning Player of the Season for 2019 before moving to Cardiff City in 2022. His brother, Joseph Itai Rinomhota, 30, reportedly died after receiving a kick to the ribs during a Muay Thai boxing match on the island of Koh Samui, Thailand.

arts.ttl: Noel Hunt has given us 'freedom to express ourselves' says Reading star
arts.sum: Reading star Charlie Savage has been full of praise for manager Noel Hunt, believing he has given the side 'freedom to express ourselves.' Savage, now a full Wales international, arrived in 2023 and is approaching 100 appearances for the club. A regular under Ruben Selles, albeit sometimes from the bench, Savage has stepped up a gear since Hunt took charge in November, scoring the third goal in Saturday's 3-1 win over Peterborough United. Now 11 matches unbeaten and in the play-offs with eight matches to go, despite another year of off-field turmoil, Savage could not speak higher of the new boss.

arts.ttl: Former Arsenal and England star watches on as Reading earn Peterborough United win
arts.sum: England legend David Seaman was in attendance at the Select Car Leasing Stadium on Saturday as Reading re-entered the play-offs with a 3-1 victory over Peterborough United. The former goalkeeper, who made over 500 appearances for Arsenal and earned 75 caps for England between 1988 and 2002, began his career with Posh. Making over 100 appearances for the London Road outfit in the early 1980s, Seaman went on to enjoy a near-20 year top-flight career. Seaman went on to move locally to Berkshire and was a special guest on Saturday as the Royals impressed against the in-form Boro, speaking to supporters in hospitality on the day. He was not the only legendary former player at the match on the day,

arts.ttl: Peterborough United chief praises Reading for 'impressive style' in statement victory
arts.sum: Peterborough United owner Darragh McAnthony praised the style of football in Reading's 3-1 victory on Saturday. The Royals, returning from the international break, ended Posh's eight match unbeaten run and extended theirs to 11 in a strong display of high-pressing football. Peterborough are marooned in midtable and have nothing to play for in League One, travelling to Wembley Stadium next month for the Vertu Trophy final, while Noel Hunt's Royals are now sixth and occupy a play-off spot with eight matches remaining. Speaking highly of the club in the build-up to the match, MacAnthony was full of praise for both sides in the aftermath of the victory, lauding the style with which both teams played.

arts.ttl: 'Another owner went missing': Former Reading forward on 'frustrating' loan move
arts.sum: Former Reading forward Billy Sharp admitted that 2013/14 was 'frustrating' as a loan extension was not possible. The 39-year-old is an EFL legend, scoring more goals in the second, third and fourth tier than anyone else. This is most prominently at clubs such as Sheffield United and Doncaster Rovers, where he is still leading the line today. Back in 2013/14, the forward was surplus to requirements at Premier League Southampton but had finished as the top scorer for Nottingham Forest in 2012/13. Moving to Berkshire in September 2013, the striker was injured shortly after arriving and had to wait until December to make his debut, scoring in a win over Charlton Athletic.
